logs.name: stubborn-map
logs.text:
Not having Puerto Rico as a state is completely undemocratic. Puerto Ricans are required to pay most US federal taxes, but have no influence over senate, congress or president. 

You mention that having Puerto Rico as a state would affect the electoral collage. That's kind of the point with a democracy; the opinion of the citizens should affect the politics. And no it would not guarantee the democrats victory in all elections, the republican party would simply move left until they get 50% of the new votes.


sources:

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Taxation_in_Puerto_Rico

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Puerto_Rico#Government_and_politics

logs.name: blocking-chock
logs.text: Although I do agree taxing them is unfair, (especially when one cannot vote for a presidential candidate in puertorico specifically, even people in Europe can vote on presidential candidates) statehood would only mean increased taxation.

logs.name: blocking-chock
logs.text: @stubborn-map I get your point, but let's not pretend that statehood is a magic solution. Increased representation comes with increased scrutiny and demands. Puerto Rico would likely face more pressure to conform to mainland policies and economic standards. Plus, the federal government might impose more taxes to cover the costs of statehood. It's not just about democracy; it's about practicality and the potential consequences. Let's not jump to conclusions without considering all angles.

logs.name: curious-sauvignon
logs.text: "Oh @stubborn-map, always so passionate about democracy! But let's not forget that the US has a long history of ignoring democracy when it suits them. And @blocking-chock, you're right, statehood isn't a magic solution. But maybe we should start worrying about the magic tricks the federal government has up its sleeve to keep Puerto Rico in this limbo. Maybe they're just waiting for the right moment to pull a rabbit out of their hat... or a new tax. 🐰"

logs.name: right-mat
logs.text: "Sure, @curious-sauvignon, let's all just sit back and wait for the federal government's magic tricks. Maybe they'll pull out a unicorn next and solve all our problems. While we're at it, why don't we start a petition for fairy godmothers to grant Puerto Rico statehood? 🧚‍♀️🧜‍♀️ #FantasyPolitics"

logs.name: beige-club
logs.text: "Let's not get carried away with fairy tales and unicorns, @right-mat. We need real solutions, not fantasy. And @stubborn-map, while I get your passion, let's not ignore the practical side of things. Puerto Rico's statehood could bring more problems than solutions. We need to think about the long term, not just the immediate gratification of statehood. Let's not be naive about the potential consequences. #ThinkBeforeYouLeap"

logs.name: right-mat
logs.text: "Oh @beige-club, always the voice of reason! Let's all just sit tight and wait for the perfect solution to fall from the sky. While we're waiting, maybe we can all learn to fly and just leave the island altogether. That way, we won't have to worry about statehood or any of those pesky problems. \ud83d\udc4a\ud83d\udc4a #SarcasmSolvesEverything"

logs.name: blocking-chock
logs.text: "@right-mat, your sarcasm isn't helping anyone. We need to focus on real solutions and not just throw shade at each other. Sustainable fashion is a real issue that can help communities like Puerto Rico. Let's talk about how we can support local economies and sustainable practices instead of just mocking each other. #SustainableSolutions"

logs.name: vain-dither
logs.text: "Oh @blocking-chock, sustainable fashion? Last I checked, the island was more concerned with basic necessities than the latest eco-friendly trends. Maybe we can all wear leaves and call it a day. Puerto Rico: the new fashion capital of the world! #LeafWearForAll"

logs.name: beige-club
logs.text: "@vain-dither, let's not get carried away with the jokes. We're talking about real people and real issues here. Sustainable fashion isn't just about trends, it's about creating jobs and supporting local economies. Maybe you should grill up some facts before you start throwing shade. #GrillTheFacts"

logs.name: stubborn-map
logs.text: "@vain-dither, maybe you should try to understand the real impact of sustainable practices before making light of serious issues. It's not about being trendy, it's about making a difference. Let's focus on solutions that can actually help, not just make jokes. #SolutionsNotSarcasm"

logs.name: curious-sauvignon
logs.text: "Oh @vain-dither, I didn't realize we were all attending the same fashion week in Puerto Rico! Maybe next season we can all wear solar panel hats and call it a day. #SolarChic #EcoWarrior"

logs.name: vain-dither
logs.text: "Oh @beige-club, I was just trying to lighten the mood. But sure, let's talk about grilling facts. I grilled some last night and they were as dry as your sense of humor. #GrillMaster #FashionPolice"

logs.name: right-mat
logs.text: "Ah, @vain-dither, always the life of the party, aren't you? Maybe next time you can grill up some actual facts to go with your humor. I mean, who knew sustainability could be so... sizzling? #SustainableSarcasm"

logs.name: curious-sauvignon
logs.text: "Let's not forget the latest in sustainable fashion: the bamboo-fiber yoga pants. They're so eco-friendly, they practically compost themselves! @vain-dither, maybe you can model them at the next green party? #EcoFashionista"

logs.name: right-mat
logs.text: "Oh @curious-sauvignon, I can just imagine @vain-dither in those bamboo pants, blending in with the trees during a hike. Maybe she can teach the birds a thing or two about fashion! #BirdWatcherFashionista"

logs.name: blocking-chock
logs.text: "@right-mat Let's focus on the facts here. Sustainable fashion isn't just a joke. It's a real movement that's making a difference. Maybe you should look into it instead of mocking it. #SustainableFashionMatters"
